title: Investor Data Room Readiness

introduction.subtitle: Operational readiness assessment. 6-8 min.
introduction.description:
Investor Data Room Readiness helps you learn the vocabulary and typical expectations behind Corporate & Ownership, Financial & Tax, Commercial & Market, IP, Technology & Risk. Use it as a private diagnostic: answer from observable evidence, not aspiration, and treat the result as a map for the next improvement cycle.

The score stays separate from the optional answer-support check, which asks whether your answers are based on opinion, anecdotes, documentation, or system-verified evidence. A high answer should mean the practice is visible, repeatable, and useful in real decisions.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.duration: 8 diagnostic questions plus 4 evidence checks. Approximately 6-8 min.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How complete and reconcilable are incorporation, share register, and cap-table records?

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Corporate & Ownership; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in Corporate & Ownership, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. Corporate & Ownership has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.
D. Corporate & Ownership is reviewed, measured over time, and strong enough to guide trade-offs under pressure.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Corporate & Ownership: How complete and reconcilable are incorporation, share register, and cap-table records
conceptHelp.summary: Corporate & Ownership means the legal identity, share records, approvals, founder agreements, and ownership facts an investor needs to trust the company structure. This question narrows the concept to "How complete and reconcilable are incorporation, share register, and cap-table records?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Corporate & Ownership matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would let an outside reviewer verify "How complete and reconcilable are incorporation, share register, and cap-table records?" from current, reconciled documents without relying on founder memory or private explanation.

questions.text: How accessible are material approvals, governance records, and contracts?

options.text: A. Authority is unclear, improvised, or concentrated in one person.
B. The practice is informal; boundaries, escalation, or records are inconsistent.
C. Owners, decision rules, and review points are documented for important cases.
D. Authority, evidence standards, exception handling, and periodic review are governed.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Corporate & Ownership: How accessible are material approvals, governance records, and contracts
conceptHelp.summary: Corporate & Ownership means the legal identity, share records, approvals, founder agreements, and ownership facts an investor needs to trust the company structure. conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would let an outside reviewer verify "How accessible are material approvals, governance records, and contracts?" from current, reconciled documents without relying on founder memory or private explanation.

questions.text: How reliable are historical financials and the operating model?

options.text: A. The answer relies mainly on opinion, pitch, memory, or isolated examples.
B. There are supporting examples, but sources are partial, informal, or hard to compare.
C. There is documented evidence from relevant customers, operations, metrics, or records.
D. The evidence is triangulated, current, owner-reviewed, and strong enough to change a material decision.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Financial & Tax: How reliable are historical financials and the operating model
conceptHelp.summary: Financial & Tax means the financial history, model, tax posture, liabilities, and assumptions that explain how the company has operated and may operate next. This question narrows the concept to "How reliable are historical financials and the operating model?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Financial & Tax matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would let an outside reviewer verify "How reliable are historical financials and the operating model?" from current, reconciled documents without relying on founder memory or private explanation.

questions.text: How well are tax, liabilities, and financial contingencies documented?

options.text: A. There is no usable model; the answer is unknown or based on rough intuition.
B. There is an estimate, but assumptions, sensitivity, and ownership are weak.
C. The model uses current inputs, documented assumptions, and regular review.
D. The model is scenario-tested, tied to triggers and decisions, and reconciled with reliable source data.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Financial & Tax: How well are tax, liabilities, and financial contingencies documented
conceptHelp.summary: Financial & Tax means the financial history, model, tax posture, liabilities, and assumptions that explain how the company has operated and may operate next. This question narrows the concept to "How well are tax, liabilities, and financial contingencies documented?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

questions.text: How quickly can a reviewer understand revenue quality and customer concentration?

options.text: A. It happens rarely, reactively, or only when a crisis forces attention.
B. It happens occasionally, but timing depends on individual initiative or urgency.
C. There is a defined cadence with clear participants, owners, and triggers.
D. The cadence is frequent enough to change decisions, includes quality checks, and improves from outcomes.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Commercial & Market: How quickly can a reviewer understand revenue quality and customer concentration
conceptHelp.summary: Commercial & Market means the quality of revenue, customers, pipeline, market evidence, and concentration risks behind the commercial story. This question narrows the concept to "How quickly can a reviewer understand revenue quality and customer concentration?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in IP, Technology & Risk; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in IP, Technology & Risk, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. IP, Technology & Risk has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.

questions.text: How transparently are security, privacy, regulatory, and litigation risks presented?

options.text: A. The area is mostly unmapped, reactive, or dependent on informal trust.
B. The area is known in broad terms, but controls, owners, or evidence are incomplete.
C. Owners, controls, thresholds, and response or remediation paths are documented.
D. Controls are tested, monitored, reviewed after incidents or changes, and integrated into operating governance.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: IP, Technology & Risk: How transparently are security, privacy, regulatory, and litigation risks presented
conceptHelp.summary: IP, Technology & Risk means ownership and risk around intellectual property, software, security, privacy, regulation, and technical dependencies. contextQuestions.text: What level of evidence supports your answers for Corporate & Ownership?

options.text: Opinion: mostly judgement, memory, or individual perception.
Anecdotal: examples exist, but they are not systematically documented.
Documented: there are current notes, models, policies, metrics, or records.
System-verified: evidence is routinely produced by a governed workflow, system, or review cadence.

conceptHelp.title: Corporate & Ownership
conceptHelp.summary: This question asks how well your answers about Corporate & Ownership are supported. Corporate & Ownership means the legal identity, share records, approvals, founder agreements, and ownership facts an investor needs to trust the company structure. The goal is learning: separate what you know from what you suspect, remember, or still need to verify.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: A result about Corporate & Ownership is more useful when you know whether it rests on opinion, a few examples, current documents, or system-verified evidence. Weak support does not make the answer useless; it tells you what to learn next.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Choose the strongest support you actually have right now. Do not upgrade an answer because the practice feels important, and do not downgrade it if current records or systems really exist.
conceptHelp.example: If your Corporate & Ownership answer is based on a recent metric, policy, deal document, customer record, or operating review, choose documented or system-verified. If it is mostly memory or judgement, choose opinion or anecdotal.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
contextDescription: This step is not scored. It records what your diagnostic answers are based on: opinion, anecdote, documentation, or system-verified evidence. Use it to decide how much to verify the result before acting on it.
